POTENTIAL_CONNECTORS.content: Sun Xiushun founded the company in 2002 [[1]], which subsequently expanded its operations into mining activities in Guinea. His firm, Winning International, has established significant partnerships with prominent entities such as China Baowu Steel Group and Chalco Iron Ore Holdings, among others, to develop West Africa’s mega mine project at Simandou [[1]]. Additionally, Sun Xiushun has been involved in collaborations with Chinese shipping and port companies like Shandong Weiqiao Group and Yantai Port Group, as well as local Guinean government officials including Alpha Condé (President of Guinea) and Mr. Wazni (Chairman of SMB), to facilitate the development of mining projects [[2] [3]].

BIOGRAPHY.content: Sun Xiushun began his career as a sailor and subsequently established a presence in Singapore's shipping sector in 2006. By developing a bauxite trade corridor between China, Singapore, and Indonesia, he positioned himself within the maritime industry before pivoting to mining and infrastructure projects in Guinea following export restrictions in Indonesia in 2013 [[1]]. In 2014, Sun co-founded the SMB-Winning consortium with the aim of developing upstream bauxite operations in Guinea [[1]].

INTERESTING_FACTS.content: Sun Xiushun started his career as a sailor and transitioned to major mining operations in West Africa, specifically in Guinea, after regulatory changes in Indonesia prompted him to pivot his business interests [[1]]. His company became involved in the Simandou project, one of the largest untapped iron ore deposits in the world, showcasing his strategic move into international mining ventures [[1]].

GIVING.content: Through his leadership, Sun Xiushun has spearheaded CSR initiatives for the SMB-Winning Consortium, which include funding community infrastructure such as two hospitals and local roads in Guinea[[1]], donating 1,540 solar panels, organizing dental health campaigns, and providing training programs for Guinean employees in China[[2]]. His commitment to enhancing healthcare and education access is evident through these initiatives.

KEY_POSITIONS.content: Sun Xiushun founded the Winning International Group and served as its Chairman, playing a pivotal role in establishing Singapore-based business interests in West Africa[[1]]. Later, he transitioned to leadership roles within the SMB-Winning Consortium, taking on the position of Chief Executive Officer and President [[2][3]]. His work has been instrumental in forming partnerships with significant Chinese companies, contributing to the development of major mining projects such as the Simandou mine in Guinea[[1][2]].

ADVERSE_NEWS.content: While there are no personal adverse news reports about Sun Xiushun, the SMB-Winning Consortium he leads has faced significant criticism and controversy regarding environmental degradation, dust pollution, and the pollution of water resources for local communities in Guinea[[1]].
